feed.title: SAP Community - SAP ERP
feed.subtitle: SAP ERP Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Compra Entrega Futura Uso/Consumo para Estoque. Erro: 8B268 - Chave de lançamento NVV não encontrada
entry.content:
Caros, bom dia!

 

Preciso do apoio de vocês para solucionar um erro que ocorre no momento da gravação do lançamento de um processo de Compra Entrega Futura de Material Uso/Consumo para Estoque (Transação MIGO movimento 801).

 

Criamos um IVA com as seguintes condições:

Imposto: V-Sup.

Utilização: 2 – Consumo

 

Condições:

ICS2 ICMS Sub.Trib.não deduzível ICST NVV 520

ZIC2 ICMS não dedutível SN ICMS NVV 517

ICON COFINS NVV imposto regular COFI NVV 780

ICOO COFINS NVV Offset imp.incl. COFI NVV 782

IPSN PIS NVV imposto regular PIS NVV 784

FCP2 ICMS FCP não Dedutível ICMS NVV 467

Imposto de compensação IPSO PIS NVV incl. PIS NVV 786

FPS2 SubTrib FCP não Dedutivo ICST NVV 471

IPI0 IPI NF linha zero IPI VST 405    

 

Ao efetuarmos o processo pelo MIGO, movimento 801, o sistema apresenta a mensagem de erro acima mencionada: “ Chave de lançamento NVV não encontrada -   Nº mensagem 8B268.

Diagnóstico: Chave de lançamento e não está definida.

Atividades do sistema:  Documento de material - erro no cálculo de imposto Lançamento de imposto necessita de chave de lançamento válida.   Verifique lançamentos automáticos para impostos em FI.”

       

Na transação OBCN, a operação NVV é definida da seguinte forma:

Denominação: IVA sup.n.ded.distr.

Tipo de imposto: 2 - IVA sup.

Não dedutível: X

Cód.lançamento: 3 - Distribuição por linhas pertinentes de despesas/receitas

Imp.s/relv.desc:

 

Os Srs. pode me ajudar a solucionar este caso?

 

Obrigada!

Angélica Souza.

entry.title: servagntprocgrp - Change Field from mandatory to optional - ECC6
entry.content:
I would like to change a Vendor Master Account group field control for (ServAgntProcGrp - LFA1-DLGRP) to make it optional, however I cannot find the field in SPRO  - Define Vendor account groups with screen layout.

Where else could this field be controlled?

entry.title: Travel Management (PR05) - Mass Update Trip Status
entry.content:
EDIT: 28/05/2026

I've found the following process: Resetting Trip Status (Recalculation Program) | SAP Help Portal

Hello,

In light of the changes to the HMRC approved mileage rate from 6th April 2026 whereby this will increase from 45p per mile to 55p per mile for the first 10,000 miles, due to the size of our payroll, we have circa. 900 individual mileage claims that have already been processed since April 2026 that will now need to be re-processed retrospectively so that they receive the revised rate. 

I am aware of transaction PRAP to approve claims and PREC to settle claims but I am not aware of any transaction or programs that can be run for mass data to amend the trip status. Is there such a thing?

In order to re-process the 900 claims we need to change the trip status back to being "Trip Completed" so that they can be re-approved and re-settled so that they receive the uplifted rate. We are trying to identify if there is a way of doing this without the need for us to do this manually via PR05 900 times. 

Thanks
Emma

entry.title: Need Clarification For EKORG
entry.content:
Hello Expert, 

HI We are  HAVING SINGLE PURCHASE ORGANIZATION AND MULTIPLE COMPANY CODE but under one company code we are having single unique plant but i want to create the single RFQ for different company code ,  how can this possible . , please guide us as because of the management needs they want single RFQ for different company codes.

entry.title: Information about SAP MM and SAP Workflow
entry.content:
Hi, I’m a student at Politecnico di Milano and I’m currently working on a university project focused on the redesign of production planning and procurement processes within a manufacturing company.

In our case study, the company already uses SAP ERP, and we are evaluating the introduction of functionalities such as SAP MM and SAP Workflow to support procurement and contract approval processes.

We were wondering whether modules like SAP MM and SAP Workflow are typically already included within a standard SAP environment, or whether they usually need to be purchased and implemented separately.

Any clarification or high-level indication would be extremely helpful for our academic project. Thank you very much for your time!

entry.title: Issue with Inbound Mail Processing in SAP
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We are facing an issue related to inbound mail processing in our SAP system.

We have already checked the following points:

  • SCOT configuration
  • SMICM service status
  • SICF service activation
  • User creation in SU01 with user type “System”
  • Network connectivity between SAP and mail server

Earlier, we were facing issues with outgoing mails as well; however, the outbound mail processing is now working properly. Despite all checks being completed successfully and no network issues being observed, inbound mails are still not being processed in SAP.

Request you to please help us identify the root cause and suggest the required solution.

Please let us know if any additional logs, traces, or configuration details are required from our end.

Regards,
Chetan Chavan

entry.title: Output Type Not Automatically Determined for Contract & Print Option Missing
entry.content:
• Output Type Not Automatically Assigned
    • While creating the Contract, the output type is not getting automatically determined/assigned.
    • Due to this, the contract output cannot be processed automatically.
  • Print / Print Preview Option Missing
    • We are also unable to find the Print or Print Preview/View option on the Contract screen.

entry.title: Programm 6PP2 nicht anlegbar
entry.content:
Hallo Zusammen, wir versuchen analog Hinweis https://me.sap.com/notes/0002368183 das Programm 6PP2 und 6PP3 anzulegen. 6PP3 hatte problemlos funktioniert, aber 6PP2 funktioniert nicht. 

Wir erhalten die Meldung im Anhang und kommen nicht weiter. 

Meldungsnummer: BL001

 

 

Danke und Grüße, Jana Häußler

entry.title: In Sales Order, Partner listed AS Assigned BP to Partner function do not shows email address
entry.content: In Sales Order, Under Partner Tab we have listed the Partners with Assigned Partner functions and Here we see all the Partners showing the Address from Master Data for Partner function but for this Business Partner we see the value in BUT000 and ADR6 the Email Address SMTP_ADDR. Attached the Sales Order Screen Email - BP screen for your reference. Kindly help me to know the enhancement where to place the select query to fullfil this change

entry.title: VF44 - Edit Revenue List - rounding
entry.content:
When performing revenue recognition through transaction VF44, a difference of 0.01 is being generated in the revenue recognition posting.

Please see the attachments

Thank you

Márcia Asada

entry.title: latest release of sap S/4 HANA database on suse 15
entry.content: i want latest releases of SAP S/4 HANA database and SAP applications on  OS suse 15

entry.title: Como reordenar colunas e alterar o layout da transação MIRO via SHD0?
entry.content:
Olá comunidade,

Estou precisando de ajuda para alterar o layout da transação MIRO e definir a exibição específica de algumas colunas.

O que eu já fiz:

  • Acessei a transação SHD0.

  • Criei uma variante de transação personalizada (iniciada com "Z").

O meu problema:

  • Não sei exatamente como configurar o formato, a visibilidade e a ordem das colunas da maneira que eu preciso dentro dessa variante de tela.

Alguém teria um passo a passo detalhado, de preferência com prints de tela, mostrando como definir a ordem e a configuração das colunas na MIRO através da SHD0?

Agradeço desde já pela ajuda!

entry.title: Incorrect Planned Order Quantity Calculation in MD11 Based on Scrap % and lot size procedure
entry.content:
The planned order created using transaction code MD11 shows an incorrect increase in order quantity based on the scrap percentage maintained in the MRP1 view of the Material Master.

Given that a fixed lot size is maintained for the material, the expectation is that the system should adjust the order quantity against the scrap percentage accordingly, without inflating the total quantity.

We observe the correct behavior when creating process orders and when planned orders are generated through the MRP/MPS run. The same behavior is expected for manually created planned orders as well.

Please find the attachment with details of test data and screenshots. Kindly look in to the issue and advise as it is impacting our project where the planned orders are created externally and posted to SAP.

entry.title: BIB Replicating departments from EC to HCM, changing the OBJID
entry.content:
So i need to replicate both Employees and Organizational Objets from SAP SF EC to SAP HCM, but i need the Ids to come from a specific field and not the IDs in SuccessFactors , for the Employee and it's PERNR i used IF_ECPAO_IN_EXT_PERNR_MAP, but now i'm trying to find a similar solution for Org Objs is there a BADi that allows that ? or any other way ?
I would really appreciate the help.

entry.title: Need Suggestions for FB60 Mass Upload (Non-PO) – BDC vs BAPI
entry.content:
Need Suggestions for FB60 Mass Upload (Non-PO) – BDC vs BAPI

 

Hi Developers,

 

I have a requirement to develop an FB60 mass Non-PO invoice uploader, where invoices are grouped based on the Reference Number.

 

I have already developed a BDC solution, and the posting process is working correctly. However, I'm facing an issue while capturing messages from the BDC execution.

 

The problem is that even when the transaction encounters an error or warning, the returned message type is sometimes 'S' (Success). Currently, I am filtering based on Message Type and Message ID to identify successfully posted documents, but this approach is not reliable in all scenarios.

 

The business users also want to see detailed error information for records that failed during upload. However, many SAP standard error messages are not user-friendly, making it difficult for end users to understand what went wrong and which field caused the issue also it has a more than 1 line error .

 

A few additional points:

 

- This is a relatively old SAP ECC 6.0 system.

- Before suggesting a BAPI solution, please note that I have already explored BAPI options.

- In my system, automatic tax code calculation is not available.

- The BAPI approach worked only for invoices without tax codes.

- I would also like to avoid manually generating the second line item that FB60 normally creates during manual entry or BDC processing.

 

My questions are:

 

1. How do you reliably identify successful and failed postings in FB60 BDC processing?

2. Is there a better way to capture and present meaningful error messages to users?

3. Has anyone successfully implemented this using a BAPI in an older ECC system, including tax handling?

4. If you have sample code or a working approach (BDC or BAPI), it would be greatly appreciated.

 

Thanks in advance for your suggestions.

entry.title: SAP Convergent Charging 2022.2 updater becomes slow/unresponsive under master data pressure
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are investigating a performance/stability issue in SAP Convergent Charging 2022.2 where the updater becomes very slow or temporarily unresponsive when pressure is applied through Web Service-based master data and provider contract operations.

Context

Landscape:

  • SAP Convergent Charging 2022.2
  • SAP CRM + SAP ERP-based BRIM integration
  • Oracle database
  • SAP CC updater used for master data/subscriber account / charging contract provisioning
  • Direct updater Web Service URL tested, without Load Balancer
  • The second updater is shut down and not part of any target group
  • Therefore, passive-updater routing and load-balancer routing are currently out of scope

Functional scenario:

  • External/client-side process creates master data and contracts in CRM
  • Business partners, contract accounts, and provider contracts are created/changed
  • These objects should be replicated/provisioned into SAP CC through the updater
  • Under pressure, the updater becomes slow and sometimes unresponsive
  • Even a simple Web Service ping/WSDL check against the direct updater URL can take more than 10 seconds, sometimes around 20 seconds

Observed behaviour

When there is no pressure:

  • Direct updater Web Service responds normally
  • Ping/WSDL checks can be fast
  • The updater appears healthy

When pressure is applied:

  • Contract/master-data creation or change operations quickly slow down
  • Updater Web Service response time suddenly increases
  • Ping/WSDL calls to the same direct updater URL start taking 10–20 seconds
  • The updater appears alive but slow/unresponsive
  • This looks like the updater Web Service processing path is blocked or saturated

What we already ruled out

  1. Load Balancer
  • The issue was reproduced using the direct updater URL
  • The second updater is shut down and not in the target group
  • Therefore, this is not caused by ALB routing to a passive updater
  1. OS limits
    The running updater process has inherited the expected limits:

Max processes:
506949

Max open files:
1048576

Max locked memory:
unlimited

  1. HugePages
    Host HugePages:

HugePages_Total: 34500
HugePages_Free: 31387
HugePages_Rsvd: 1116
Hugepagesize: 2048 kB
Hugetlb: 70656000 kB

Updater process HugePages:

Private_Hugetlb: 6375424 kB
HugetlbPages: 6375424 kB
VmSwap: 0 kB
Threads: 112
VmRSS: 667600 kB

Interpretation:

  • HugePages are active
  • HugePages are not exhausted
  • The updater is not swapping
  • Process/thread/file limits do not look like the cause
  1. JVM/GC/host pressure from previous measurements
    Previous load windows did not show:
  • Full GC pressure
  • Heap exhaustion
  • Swap usage
  • CPU saturation
  • Local disk saturation

Current updater-relevant parameters

Updater Web Service / SQL / diagnostics settings:

WS_SERVER_THREAD_COUNT = 16
WS_SERVER_QUEUE_SIZE = computed
SQLHELPER_CONNECTION_COUNT = 64
SQLHELPER_DB_TIMEOUT = 0
SQLHELPER_RECONNECT_ATTEMPTS = 5
SQLHELPER_RECONNECT_INTERVAL = 10
SQLHELPER_STATEMENT_CACHE_SIZE = 1

THREAD_DUMP_ENABLED = false
THREAD_DUMP_THRESHOLD_SQL_TIME = 0
THREAD_DUMP_TRIGGER_PERIOD = 0
THREAD_DUMP_OUTPUT_PATH = work/dump
THREAD_DUMP_MINIMUM_INTERVAL = 60

Audit is enabled:

AUDIT_ACTIVATION = true
AUDIT_OBJECT_ACTIVATION = true
AUDIT_DOMAIN = CATALOG, CUSTOMER, TECHNICAL, ADMINISTRATION
AUDIT_OBJECT_DOMAIN = CATALOG,CATALOG_TABLE_DATA,CUSTOMER,CUSTOMER_TABLE_DATA,TECHNICAL

Current hypothesis

Because the direct updater Web Service URL becomes slow under pressure, and OS/HugePages/GC/CPU/disk do not show clear pressure, we suspect one of the following:

  1. All updater Web Service worker threads become occupied
  2. WS requests wait in the updater Web Service queue
  3. WS workers are blocked on Oracle DB wait / DB lock / slow SQL / SQL reconnect
  4. Audit/object-audit database writes are slowing down provisioning operations
  5. Internal updater lock contention occurs inside the Java/SAP CC runtime
  6. A small number of long-running provisioning calls block the available Web Service worker pool, causing even simple ping/WSDL requests to wait

Questions

  1. In SAP CC 2022.2, is it expected that if all updater Web Service worker threads are occupied, even simple Web Service ping/WSDL calls become slow because they wait behind the same Web Service worker/queue mechanism?
  2. For CRM/ERP-driven master data and provider contract provisioning, which SAP CC updater indicators are the most useful to prove Web Service worker saturation or queueing?
  3. Is there a recommended way to monitor the active usage of WS_SERVER_THREAD_COUNT and WS_SERVER_QUEUE_SIZE on the updater?
  4. Could AUDIT_ACTIVATION=true and AUDIT_OBJECT_ACTIVATION=true significantly slow down mass creation/change of customer master data and provider contracts in the updater?
  5. Which database tables or Oracle waits should be checked first for updater provisioning pressure, especially around audit/object snapshots and provider contract creation?
  6. Is SQLHELPER_DB_TIMEOUT=0 acceptable for this type of high-volume provisioning scenario, or should DB connectivity/SQL wait detection be configured differently?
  7. Is SQLHELPER_STATEMENT_CACHE_SIZE=1 a known bottleneck for updater-heavy provisioning workloads, or should this only be changed under SAP guidance?

  8. For this symptom, the correct next step is to enable:

    • THREAD_DUMP_ENABLED=true
    • THREAD_DUMP_THRESHOLD_SQL_TIME=5000
    • THREAD_DUMP_MINIMUM_INTERVAL=60
    • THREAD_DUMP_OUTPUT_PATH=work/dump
    • THREAD_DUMP_TRIGGER_PERIOD=0

    Does SAP CC generate a thread dump when an SQL query exceeds 5 seconds?

  9. In addition to automatic thread dumps, should we also capture manual jstack dumps exactly when the updater Web Service ping takes 10–20 seconds?
  10. Are there known SAP Notes, configuration recommendations, or best practices for updater pressure during CRM/ERP master data replication and provider contract provisioning in SAP CC 2022.2?

The evidence we plan to collect during the next slowdown

At the exact moment when the updater Web Service ping takes 10–20 seconds:

Admin+:

server status updater
get SERVICES_STATISTICS_MEMORY updater
get WS_SERVER_THREAD_COUNT MEMORY updater
get WS_SERVER_QUEUE_SIZE MEMORY updater
get SQLHELPER_CONNECTION_COUNT MEMORY updater
get FREE_MEMORY MEMORY updater
get TOTAL_MEMORY MEMORY updater

OS/JVM:

cat /proc/<updater_pid>/status
cat /proc/<updater_pid>/limits
cat /proc/<updater_pid>/smaps_rollup
jstack -l <updater_pid>

Socket/file evidence:

ss -tanp
lsof -p <updater_pid>

Oracle:

v$session wait events
blocking sessions
long-running SQL
top SQL by elapsed time
audit/object audit table pressure

The main question is:

What is the correct SAP CC way to prove whether the updater collapse is caused by Web Service thread saturation, DB wait/lock, audit/object-audit persistence, SQL reconnect/wait, or internal Java/updater lock contention?
SAP Convergent Charging

entry.title: Expired Useful Life” and “Expired Periods”
entry.content:
We have observed that the fields “Expired Useful Life” and “Expired Periods” are not displayed in the asset master for asset 280036, as the asset was capitalized in the current fiscal year.

However, for asset 280033, these fields are visible since the asset capitalization occurred in prior fiscal years, where depreciation periods have already elapsed.

entry.title: Sort component in the production form as per CS12
entry.content: In the production form components are sorted as per production order only, but business need component sorted as per CS12

entry.title: ABAP list based PI Sheets on S/4 Hana Cloud Private edition
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We are currently in the process of a digital transformation from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA and require clarification regarding ABAP list-based PI Sheets as we use them in our current design.

As part of our assessment, we do not see clear SAP Notes explicitly confirming the support status of ABAP list-based PI Sheets in S/4HANA.

We would like to understand:

  • Whether ABAP list-based PI Sheets (Control Recipe Destination Type 1) are supported in SAP S/4HANA.
  •  Request SAP Notes or official documentation confirming the above

I request your confirmation and any relevant OSS Notes or simplification references to support our migration planning.

Thanks & Regards

Ashwin

entry.title: Price of goods mvt will become negative in goods receipt for PO
entry.content:
when the time of Goods receipt for Purchase order getting error " Price of goods mvt will become negative"

in GR there are multiple line items, all other line items are working fine but for Line item 30 getting error "Price of goods mvt will become negative"

after checking line item, there is no mismatch of GR and IR, but values 14,524.720 and quantities 14,524.720 are same. 

Please check and suggest the solution.
